items.summary: Every weekday, the Investing Club releases the Homestretch; an actionable afternoon update just in time for the last hour of trading.

items.headline: Swiss sneaker maker On Holding shakes up leadership amid slowing growth
items.summary: Co-founders to become co-CEOs as On prepares to enter its "next growth phase," the company said in a statement Wednesday.

items.headline: Jury in Los Angeles finds Meta, YouTube negligent in social media addiction trial
items.summary: A jury reached a verdict in the LA trial focused on Meta and YouTube, a case that experts have characterized as social media's "Big Tobacco" moment.

items.headline: Treasury yields tumble as Trump talks up Iran ceasefire plan
items.summary: U.S. Treasury yields ticked lower on Wednesday amid hopes of a potential peace plan in the Middle East.

items.headline: Republicans balk at Senate Democrats' DHS shutdown counteroffer
items.summary: Congress and the White House are working to reach a funding deal before Congress leaves for a scheduled two-week recess on Friday.

items.headline: Trump administration waives gasoline regulations to address surging fuel prices
items.summary: EPA Administrator Lee Zeldin said the waivers will help prevent a fuel supply disruption in the U.S. during the Iran war.

items.headline: White House says Trump will meet Xi in China in May
items.summary: A long-awaited meeting between President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping will take place in Beijing on May 14 and 15, the White House said.

items.headline: Space stocks rally on reports of SpaceX's imminent IPO filing
items.summary: Space stocks, including AST SpaceMobile, Rocket Lab and Firefly Aerospace, rallied after a report that SpaceX may file to go public this week.

items.headline: I don’t want Social Security to switch me from disability to retirement benefits. How can I stop it?
items.summary: “I want to wait until 70 to collect full retirement benefits.”

items.headline: Chewy says pet ownership to keep increasing despite worries about rising costs
items.summary: The online pet-supplies retailer’s stock is having its best day in two years as it’s selling more pet-care items and provided an upbeat sales outlook.

items.headline: Microsoft's diversity chief is leaving as company continues 'AI-powered transformation'
items.summary: Hiring and retaining top talent is becoming more critical as Microsoft seeks to develop top-class artificial intelligence models and products.

items.headline: Prediction markets' new insider trading restrictions aren't enough, bipartisan senators say
items.summary: Sens. Adam Schiff, D-Calif., and John Curtis, R-Utah, said they are optimistic their bill has enough bipartisan support to pass in Congress.

items.headline: Trump says he could send National Guard to airports 'for more help'
items.summary: President Donald Trump says he is considering sending the National Guard to airports where TSA officer absences have led to long lines.

items.headline: As March Madness unfolds, NY Fed highlights sports betting toll on consumer credit health
items.summary: As sports gambling becomes more popular and less restricted, consumer credit health is suffering, according to a new report by the New York Fed.

items.headline: The surprising reason Americans are working longer than they used to
items.summary: Older workers today have more wealth than previous generations but are afraid to use it.

items.headline: Here’s what airlines are offering passengers trapped in long TSA lines — and why travelers won’t get much else
items.summary: As travelers continue to face hours-long airport security lines during the busy spring-break season, some airlines are making it easier and less expensive to change flights.

items.headline: U.S. brought back $100 million of gold from Venezuela, Interior Secretary Burgum says
items.summary: The Interior secretary said Venezuela's interim president, Delcy Rodriguez, wants foreign investment in the country's mining sector.

items.headline: Mortgage rates near 6-month high — but here’s how much worse it would be without Freddie and Fannie’s bond buying
items.summary: The spike in U.S. mortgage rates since the Iran war began in late February has been dashing hopes for an affordability boost ahead of the spring home-buying season.

items.headline: Palantir could be a big winner of Trump’s $185 billion ‘Golden Dome’
items.summary: Software is critical to the missile-defense program, and that may mean “many” billions of dollars worth of new business for Palantir.

items.headline: Iran rejects U.S. ceasefire offer, demands sovereignty over Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: President Donald Trump has claimed the U.S. and Iran are "in negotiations now." But the Islamic Republic denies any direct talks to end the war.

items.headline: Meta cutting several hundred jobs across Reality Labs, Facebook and other departments
items.summary: The layoffs come as Meta has been refocusing its efforts and pouring billions of dollars into AI.

items.headline: Oil pares losses as traders weigh cease-fire prospects and try to ‘price in light at the end of the tunnel’
items.summary: Traders are watching the clock on President Trump’s five-day pause on energy-infrastructure attacks.

items.headline: European markets finish higher as U.S. seeks end to Iran conflict
items.summary: European stocks advanced Wednesday as the U.S. showed more signs it was trying to de-escalate the war with Iran.

items.headline: Here’s how much it could cost to fix Mideast oil and gas production damaged by the Iran war
items.summary: The damage to energy infrastructure in the Middle East caused by the war with Iran will take years and billions of dollars to repair, according to Rystad analysts.

items.headline: ‘We’re living the simple life’: I was a fisherman and my wife was a nurse. We retired with $6 million. Here’s how we did it.
items.summary: “It probably makes little sense, given our lifestyle, to obsess over retirement accounts.”

items.headline: Gen Z brothers bought a $4,000 pickup truck to start their business—now it brings in $3 million a year
items.summary: Kirk and Jacob McKinney found entrepreneurial inspiration at a local dump, launching junk removal and reselling business Junk Teens while still in high school.

items.headline: The research firm whose AI paper knocked the whole stock market is out with another big call

items.headline: ‘This is a first-world problem’: I can’t roll over my $800,000 401(k) from my prior employer. What did I do wrong?
items.summary: “I am frustrated.”

items.headline: New Mexico seeking changes to Meta's platform after jury finds company liable
items.summary: New Mexico Attorney General Raúl Torrez plans to ask for algorithm and design changes, age verification and independent verification in the trial's next phase.

items.headline: Arm jumps 20% as company expects revenue windfall from new chip, a 'significant shift'
items.summary: Arm said it expects its new chip to generate six times more revenue in 2031 than the $4 billion it made in 2025.

items.headline: UK government to trial social media ban for hundreds of teens
items.summary: A teen social media ban pilot in the U.K. will include restrictions ranging from curfews to time caps on popular apps like Instagram, TikTok, and Snapchat.

items.headline: Former SEC Chair Jay Clayton says regulators would scrutinize trading ahead of Trump post
items.summary: "Any move like that in advance of any announcement, the regulators are going to look at," said Clayton, former SEC chair.

items.headline: It’s not just oil and gas. The Strait of Hormuz blockage is rattling another vital commodity

items.headline: Merck to buy Terns Pharmaceuticals for $6.7 billion to boost cancer pipeline
items.summary: Merck is bulking up its portfolio ahead of its best-selling cancer drug Keytruda losing patent protection in 2028.

items.headline: Watch Interior Secretary Burgum address oil industry as Trump hints at negotiated end to Iran war
items.summary: Investors will be looking for updates from Burgum, who also chairs Trump's energy dominance council, on the state of the U.S. war against Iran

items.headline: ECB ready to hike rates even if expected inflation surge is short-lived, Lagarde says
items.summary: The ECB kept interest rates on hold at its last monetary policy meeting last week.

items.headline: Oil giants raise the alarm over energy shortages as Iran war drags on
items.summary: Shell CEO Wael Sawan warns Europe will be next to face an energy supply crunch.

items.headline: Recession odds climb on Wall Street as economy shows cracks beneath the surface
items.summary: Economists have pulled up their risk assessments of a contraction amid heightened uncertainty over geopolitical risk and a labor market slump.

items.headline: Pop Mart shares plunge over 22% as concerns over sustainability of Labubu sales dwarf stellar results
items.summary: the Beijing-based toy maker posted annual revenue of 37.1 billion yuan ($5.4 billion) for 2025, up 185% from a year earlier, just shy of LSEG estimates of 38 billion yuan.

items.headline: SK Hynix files confidentially for U.S. listing as it rides 'unprecedented growth' in memory market

items.headline: Legal AI startup Harvey valued at $11 billion in funding round, as VCs spread bets beyond model companies
items.summary: Harvey will use its fresh capital to expand its AI agents and grow its embedded legal engineering teams.

items.headline: Hugo Barra's return to Meta 5 years after exit underscores Zuckerberg's AI urgency
items.summary: During Hugo Barra's first stint at Meta, the company was pushing heavily into VR. Now, he's back and the focus is AI agents.

items.summary: Mortgage demand continued to tumble last week, as mortgage rates surged higher and affordability weakened further.

items.headline: Baltimore is first U.S. city to sue over Grok deepfake porn as legal pressure mounts on Musk's xAI

items.headline: Meta must pay $375 million for violating New Mexico law in child exploitation case, jury rules

items.headline: Iran has received Trump's 15-point plan to end war, report says; President says 'in negotiations right now'
items.summary: It comes amid a messaging clash between the U.S. and Iran on peace negotiations.

items.headline: South Korea braces for 'worst-case scenarios' as Iran oil shock deepens
items.summary: South Korean Prime Minister Kim Min-seok warned the government must prepare for "worst-case scenarios" stemming from the Middle East conflict.

items.headline: The Gulf built a global events empire, now war is putting it on ice
items.summary: As war grounds flights and threatens to destabilize the region's carefully built future, events from cultural to crypto have been quietly rescheduled.

items.summary: The fallout from the sprawling Middle East conflict is expected to represent a watershed moment for the energy transition.

items.headline: Denmark’s PM Frederiksen suffers election setback after standing up to Trump over Greenland
items.summary: Frederiksen had sought to capitalize on popular support after standing up to Trump's push to annex Greenland, a self-governing Danish territory.

items.headline: Meta makes 'big bet' on top leaders with stock options as pressure builds to catch up in AI
items.summary: Meta is granting stock options to top executives as pressure on the company intensifies in the AI battle.

items.headline: Arm stock pops 6% as CEO Haas issues $15 billion revenue expectation for new chip
items.summary: Arm unveiled its first in-house chip on Tuesday at an event in San Francisco, with Meta as the initial customer.

items.headline: Private credit’s ‘zero-loss fantasy’ is coming to an end as defaults and fund exits rise
items.summary: Private credit is expected to see a surge in defaults as investors continue to pull money from the sector.

items.headline: Don't be fooled by the UK's pre-war inflation print — a 'brutal' surge could be coming

items.headline: What comes after Labubu? Inside Pop Mart’s next grow play

items.headline: Circle posts worst day on record as proposed law could limit stablecoin yield

items.headline: Judge presses DOD on why Anthropic was blacklisted: 'That seems a pretty low bar'
items.summary: The Defense Department designated Anthropic as a risk to U.S. national security, the first time an American company had been hit with that designation.

items.headline: Amazon acquires 'approachable' humanoid maker Fauna Robotics
items.summary: Fauna's first product, called Sprout, is a $50,000 bipedal robot that's 3 feet, 6 inches tall and designed to be "approachable and human-friendly."

items.headline: OpenAI shutters short-form video app Sora as company reels in costs
items.summary: OpenAI said it's closing Sora, the short-form video app that went viral after its launch six months ago

items.headline: Databricks enters cybersecurity market with Lakewatch launch, bulking up ahead of IPO
items.summary: Databricks wants to draw on AI to help organizations respond to a higher speed of attacks, based on newly disclosed vulnerabilities.

items.headline: Micron stock sinks for a fourth straight day despite dominant earnings report
items.summary: Micron's revenue almost tripled in the last quarter, but the memory company's stock has sunk about 15% since it reported earnings on Wednesday.

items.headline: Dimon warns on AI job losses, calls for government-business incentives
items.summary: The potential AI-related disruption of the job market is a critical issue in Washington, with lawmakers trying to curb the amount of displacement.

items.headline: Google partners with Agile Robots, growing its AI robotics footprint
items.summary: Google's DeepMind division has been partnering with more robotics companies in recent months.

items.headline: OpenAI revamps shopping experience in ChatGPT after struggling with Instant Checkout offering
items.summary: OpenAI said the new experience will make it easier for users to find and compare products.

items.headline: Arm releases first in-house chip, with Meta as debut customer
items.summary: Arm is making its first CPU, pivoting from only licensing its architecture to giants like Apple, Nvidia, Amazon and Google. CNBC got an exclusive first look.

items.headline: We're buying more of this cybersecurity stock as the market keeps getting it wrong
items.summary: We've repeatedly pointed out that we do not think AI will disintermediate traditional cybersecurity vendors.

items.headline: Anthropic says Claude can now use your computer to finish tasks for you in AI agent push
items.summary: Anthropic and its rivals are trying to ramp up capabilities of AI agents after OpenClaw went viral earlier this year.

items.headline: Gap says it will launch checkout within Google's Gemini, in an AI first from a major fashion company
items.summary: Gap's partnership with Gemini and its customer-facing AI tools give it a competitive edge at a time when winning in specialty retail is harder than ever.

items.headline: Amazon's Zoox to debut robotaxis in Austin, Miami later this year as it awaits paid ride approval
items.summary: It marks the latest expansion for Amazon's Zoox self-driving unit, which opened rides to the public in some parts of Las Vegas and San Francisco last year.

items.headline: Alibaba reveals new AI chip designed for 'agents'
items.summary: While much of the attention has focused on GPUs, an area Nvidia dominates, CPUs are seen as key for AI inferencing and agents.
